title: Tulotis whangshanensis
intro: Tulotis whangshanensis, also known as the Whangshan Tulotis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tulotis whangshanensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Tulotis whangshanensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, teas, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Tulotis whangshanensis is classified within the family Fabaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within Fabaceae. The genus Tulotis is a small group of flowering plants, and T. whangshanensis is one of the species within this genus. Further classification includes the tribe Millettieae and the subtribe Millettinae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics.
